channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Paris tourist attractions reduce opening hours as extreme heat grips much of France
item.description: Several of Paris's best-known tourist attractions have shortened their opening hours as France faces its third heat wave since May. The Eiffel Tower announced that it would close early over the weeken...

item.title: Meta Discontinues AI Image Feature Amid Privacy Concerns
item.description: Meta Platforms has decided to discontinue its Muse Image AI feature, which allowed users to generate images using public Instagram accounts, just days after its launch. The decision comes amid widespr...

item.title: Justin Stamper's Real Estate Empire and TV Success
item.description: Justin Stamper, an American real estate investor and television personality, rose to fame through his work on A&E's Zombie House Flipping. He started flipping properties at 19 after saving his family'...

item.title: Hamilton Bus Thief Sentenced to Nine Months for Mental Health Treatment and Probation
item.description: A 36-year-old man who stole a Hamilton city bus during a joy ride and left passengers on board has been sentenced to nine months in jail. The court recommended he receive mental health treatment at a ...

item.title: David Beckham's global influence and business empire
item.description: The article explores David Beckham's transformation from a footballer to a global business icon, highlighting his strategic brand expansion and cultural impact. It details his role in the 2026 World C...

item.title: South African football mourns passing of star Jayden Adams
item.description: The South African football community is in profound mourning following the sudden death of Jayden Adams, a key midfielder for Bafana Bafana and Mamelodi Sundowns. The 25-year-old player was found dece...

item.title: NRL Round 19 highlights: Roosters edge out Eels in thrilling clash
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald reports on the NRL Round 19 match between the Sydney Roosters and the Parramatta Eels, which took place on July 11, 2026. The game, held at the Roosters' home ground, saw a c...

item.title: Critics question public spending on Vijayanagar's air-conditioned bus shelter
item.description: The article discusses the construction of an air-conditioned bus shelter at Vijayanagar bus stop, which has drawn criticism from commuters and local activists. Despite the development, they argue that...

item.title: Western Kenya Leaders Back Oparanya as Ruto's Deputy Candidate
item.description: Western Kenya leaders are pushing for Wycliffe Oparanya to be President William Ruto's running mate in the 2027 elections, with Khwisero MP Christopher Aseka leading the call. Aseka argues that ODM's ...

item.title: Oyo State Governor Announces 48-Hour Medical Observation for Rescued Oriire Students and Teachers
item.description: Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde has announced that the 39 pupils and five teachers rescued from captivity in Oriire will undergo medical observation for 48 hours before being handed over to state auth...

item.title: Two Ambushes in Cotabato City Result in Three Deaths
item.description: Cotabato City experienced two separate ambush attacks within a seven-hour span on July 11, 2026, resulting in three fatalities and several injuries. The first incident occurred before dawn in Barangay...

item.title: Tragedy in Vietnam: 15 Indian Tourists Killed in Speedboat Accident
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Vietnam when a speedboat capsized, resulting in the deaths of 15 Indian tourists. The incident, which took place in the coastal region, has raised concerns about maritime...

item.title: National Liquor Traders Call for Industry-Wide Collaboration to Address Safety Concerns Post-Enyobeni Inquest
item.description: The National Liquor Traders (NLT) have called for urgent collaboration between government regulators, law enforcement agencies, and the liquor industry to address systemic safety failures following th...

item.title: Gwadar Port Launches First Commercial Bunkering Operation
item.description: Gwadar Port has successfully conducted its first commercial bunkering operation, marking a significant milestone in expanding its maritime services. The operation, carried out between July 9 and 11, i...

item.title: Peller and Jarvis Prepare for White Wedding After Traditional Ceremony
item.description: The article discusses the announcement of a celebrity wedding between Peller and Jarvis, two prominent Nigerian digital creators. It highlights their journey from rising influencers to becoming a cult...

item.title: World Cup quarter-finals: England vs Norway, Argentina vs Switzerland
item.description: England and Norway are set to clash in the World Cup quarter-finals, with Harry Kane leading the charge for the Three Lions. Kane's six goals have propelled England past Mexico, despite a red card, wh...

item.title: Young Filipino Student Shines in International English Competition
item.description: A Grade 1 Filipino student, Renvin Michael Bernardo, has made headlines by winning the Macau International English Competition with a perfect score. Representing the Mathematics Trainers’ Guild Phils....

item.title: Joyang wins 500,000 pesos in Kapamilya Deal or No Deal
item.description: The news article reports on Joyang's participation in the Philippine television game show 'Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal,' where they successfully secured a prize of 500,000 pesos. The piece highlights th...

item.title: Historical Runaway Slave Ads Reveal 19th Century Struggles for Freedom
item.description: The article explores the historical context of runaway slave advertisements from the 19th century, highlighting their role in documenting the systemic oppression of slavery. These notices, often publi...

item.title: Marine archaeologists search for 1883 SS Erie Belle wreck in Lake Huron
item.description: A group of marine archaeologists and volunteers will conduct an underwater survey near Boiler Beach in Ontario this summer to investigate the 1883 explosion of the SS Erie Belle steam tug. The ship, w...
